title: Tabbed Blueprint Project Workspace
concept: A WordPress-admin-inspired Playground workspace where each Playground is an open project tab, and the selected project exposes Site, Files, Blueprint, Database, Logs, and Settings as within-project tabs. The first viewport centers Blueprint authoring while keeping launch routes, saved identity, destructive impacts, and portability outcomes visible.
focus: project tabs for temporary, saved, and PR-backed Playgrounds
Blueprint-author-first workspace with catalog, editor, run, inspect, copy, and bundle controls
left navigation for distinct launch routes and saved Playground management
explicit save destination, reset, delete, import, and export consequences
flows: Start from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or Import .zip with route-specific inputs
Save an unsaved Playground to browser storage or a local directory, show file-copy progress, then show the saved project identity
Manage saved and unsaved Playgrounds with project tabs, saved list, rename, delete, and destructive state warnings
Author and run a Blueprint from blueprint.json, inspect a selected gallery entry, copy a Blueprint link, and download a bundle
Use Site Manager tabs for live site navigation, file browser, Blueprint editor, database tools, logs, settings reset/reload, export to GitHub, and download as zip
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
select featured Blueprint shortcuts and inspect details
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
edit WordPress, PHP, and language settings
understand destructive reset and reload behavior
use File browser with create folder, create file, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
inspect logs and empty or informational states
